articles.title: 70 km wind, heavy rain, thunderstorms and...: Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, West Bengal, Punjab, Haryana, Assam, Bihar, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh to..., check full IMD forecast here - news24online.com
articles.full_text:
70 km wind, heavy rain, thunderstorms and…: Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, West Bengal, Punjab, Haryana, Assam, Bihar, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh to…, check full IMD forecast here
IMD Weather Update: Amidst the sweltering heat, over 10 states across India are expected to get relief in the coming days. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned against heavy rainfall and thunderstorms in several regions. Meanwhile, the monsoon is scheduled to hit the Kerala coast on May 26, bringing major relief from the ongoing heatwave in some states. However, the Met Department has also issued an alert for strong surface winds during this period, with speeds reaching up to 70 kmph.
IMD Weather Update: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has predicted heatwave to severe heatwave conditions over the plains of central and northwest India during this week. The weather is, however, expected to see a shift in pattern as a heavy rainfall alert has been issued for northeastern states, including Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, and Tripura. The other affected states likely to see a similar activity during the next 6 to 7 days include the Andaman & Nicobar Islands and Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry & Karaikal, Kerala & Mahe, Lakshadweep, and South Interior Karnataka. (Image: Gemini)
How will the weather be in Delhi-NCR for next 6 days?
A gradual rise in maximum temperatures by about 2°C is expected in Delhi-NCR for the next 2 days, and no large change thereafter. IMD has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions at isolated places until May 22, with sustained strong surface winds reaching 20 to 30 kmph and occasionally gusting to 40 kmph on May 21. Thereafter, the sky will remain mainly clear. (Image: Gemini)
How will the weather be in Uttar Pradesh today?
Residents of Uttar Pradesh are likely to see rain at isolated places in Meerut, Kanpur, Agra, Mathura, Aligarh, Gorakhpur, Banda, Rae Bareli, Varanasi, Maharajganj, Deoria, and Mau on Tuesday. During this period, winds are expected to blow at speeds of 30 to 40 kilometers per hour. (Image: Gemini)
How will the weather be in West Bengal, Odisha, and Bihar?
Light rainfall accompanied by thunderstorm, lightning, gusty winds (speed reaching 40-50 kmph and gusting to 70 kmph) is likely over Andaman & Nicobar Islands during 19th-24th; Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim during 20th – 23rd; Gangetic West Bengal during 21st and 22nd; Bihar during 22nd-24th; Odisha on 19th and during 22nd-24th. (Image: Gemini)
How will the weather be in Punjab, Haryana, Uttarakhand and Himachal?
Isolated light to moderate rainfall with thunderstorm, lightning, and gusty winds (speed reaching 40-50 kmph) is likely over Jammu-Kashmir during 19th -22nd; Uttarakhand during 20th-22nd May; Himachal Pradesh on 21st and 22nd May. Meanwhile, strong surface winds (speed reaching 20-30 kmph gusting to 40 kmph) are also likely over Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, and Uttar Pradesh during 19th-24th May. (Image: Gemini)
Hailstorms, rain, thunderstorms, and lightning to hit South Peninsular India
Rain accompanied by thunderstorm, lightning, gusty winds (speed reaching 50-60 kmph) is likely over Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Yanam & Rayalaseema during 20th-22nd; Lakshadweep, during 19th-22nd; Coastal Karnataka on 19th; Telangana during 19th-20th May; Tamil Nadu, Puducherry & Karaikal, Kerala & Mahe on 19th. (Image: Gemini)
